behavior_summary: Implements in-game inspection and use behavior for potion items. POTION::look displays a short description ('potion ') to the viewer.

POTION::use dispatches behavior by the potion's frame (a subtype index). Depending on frame it: reports empty, heals (large or small), restores mana, applies temporary status/buffs (including an enrage/immortal window), applies a stasis/sleep effect on the avatar, or spawns helper routines to manage multi-step visual/sound/effect sequences.

Several effects compute amounts/durations from NPC stats (Str, Int) and urandom-generated randomness, update the NPC HP/Mana, set/clear item frame to mark consumption, and spawn engine methods/processes for visuals, SFX, and timed cleanup.
notable_control_flow: Main entry (Event 0) is POTION::look which is a short, single-path handler that barks 'potion ' to the viewer and returns.
Use handler branches on Item::getFrame() (local 'frame') to select one of multiple potion behaviors — this switch is the central control flow pivot.

Some potion effects perform immediate state changes (set HP/Mana, setFrame to mark empty) while others spawn helper processes (U8P_POTION::POTION::059B, U8P_POTION::POTION::0687) that implement timed or multi-step behavior (visuals, immortal window, clearing flags after delay).
Many branches call Item::bark to communicate results (messages like 'it's empty...', 'I feel much better!', 'Yummm...', 'Zzzzz...'), then either finish or schedule further actions via spawn.
Timed behaviors often compute a duration from Npc::getInt() or Npc::getStr() (commonly doubled) and use spawn/suspend patterns to wait and then reverse temporary flags (e.g., Npc::clrImmortal).
open_questions: Exact game-visible labels for each numeric frame value (only inferred from behavior; e.g., frame 1 = strong heal or harmful if over-heal, frame 3 = weaker heal, frame 2 = mana potion, frame 5 = berserk/enrage, frame 6 = stasis/sleep, frame 7 = complex SFX/status) — the canonical mapping of subtype->name is not present.

Precise semantics of engine METHODS invoked (METHOD::0113, METHOD::015E7, METHOD::0080, METHOD::091C, FREE::1C19, FREE::29D8): appear to create/position visuals and free resources, but exact visual/effect semantics remain uncertain.
Exact status bits tested and set by Item::getStatus interactions in POTION::0687 (which bits correspond to what gameplay flag).

Whether some healing branches can actively harm an actor (inferred from a path that reduces HP and barks an agonal message) and the exact condition triggering that harm is inferred but not fully certain.

main_function.name: U8P_POTION::look
main_function.purpose: Handle an inspect/U8P_POTION::look event for a potion item; produce the short description/bark directed at the viewer.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_POTION::look/examine) — called when an actor views/inspects the potion (viewer pid passed implicitly).

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::look
function_map.role: Inspect/describe the potion to the viewer (Event 0).

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::use
function_map.role: Primary use handler (Event 1). Branches by Item::getFrame() to implement different potion subtypes (healing, mana, stasis, buffs, spawn helpers).

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::059B
function_map.role: Spawned helper for a berserk/enrage-type potion: set item used, bark, apply temporary immortal/combat buff, spawn visual effects, wait, then clear buff and free effects.

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::0687
function_map.role: Spawned helper for a complex/status SFX potion: plays sound, inspects/sets item status bits, calls engine methods to create/position effects, computes duration from Int, and schedules cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided call used to display short text messages from an item/actor to a viewer/process.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a helper process to implement a temporary berserk/enrage effect (visuals, immortal window, timed duration).
Spawn a helper process to implement a complex multi-step SFX/status routine (sound, flags, timed cleanup).

external_calls.purpose: Display the short description string 'potion ' to the viewing process/pid.
Read potion subtype/frame to decide which effect to apply.
Clear or change the potion frame to mark it used/consumed or transition state after use.
Read current HP to compute healing or conditional harmful effects.
Apply new HP value after healing or damage calculations.
Supply Strength stat to scale healing/damage amounts (commonly multiplied by 2).
Produce small random offsets for healed/restored amounts or durations.
Read current Mana for mana-restoring potion branches.
Use Intelligence to scale mana restoration or effect durations (often doubled).
Apply new Mana value after restoration calculations.
Start auxiliary processes for visuals, SFX, helper routines, and timed cleanup (e.g., spawn FREE::1C19, METHOD::* or helper functions).
Set the NPC immortal for a temporary buff duration to prevent death during the effect.
Clear the immortal flag after the effect duration expires.
Play sound effect(s) associated with the potion's status/SFX routine.
Place the avatar/player in stasis/immobilized state (used by the stasis/sleep-type potion).
Read actor facing direction to orient spawned visual effects.
Inspect item status bits to decide conditional behaviors in some complex potion branches (notably POTION::0687).
Invoke an engine method likely used to create/position visuals or effect actors tied to potion use.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements in-game inspection and use behavior for potion items. POTION::look displays a short description ('potion ') to the viewer.
